reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
Tuesday in late May. No holiday, no milestone, no one watching. Just a regular weekday with regular work, regular traffic, regular dinner to figure out. This is the day that used to feel unbearable to me — the flat middle of the week, where there was no occasion to drink to and no occasion to recover from, just the hum of being alive without anything to dress it up.

What I didn't understand at 22, or even at 29 when I finally put it down, is that the ordinary Tuesday is the whole game. The big days take care of themselves — adrenaline carries you. It's the unremarkable afternoons that build a life or quietly dismantle one. The coffee you actually taste. The text you answer instead of avoid. The walk after work instead of the bottle. Nothing about it looks like recovery from the outside. From the inside, it's everything.

Durant's line about Aristotle gets quoted to death because it's true. You don't become someone you can live with in a single brave moment. You become that person on a Tuesday in May, doing one small honest thing, then another. Eleven years in, I can tell you: the days I'm most grateful for aren't the ski trips or the summits. They're the quiet ones I used to think I couldn't survive. Turns out they were the ones I was built for.
reflection.meditation: Sit for two minutes today and notice one ordinary thing — the weight of your mug, the sound outside, your own breathing. That noticing is the practice.

affirmations: I am building my life in the small choices, not the big ones.
Today I can be present for an ordinary day without needing to escape it.
I am someone who shows up — for the work, for the people, for myself.

gratitude.prayer: I'm grateful for a Tuesday I don't have to run from. For the fact that I woke up clear-headed, with my own memory of yesterday intact and my own plans for today. For the people who stayed in my corner when I gave them every reason not to, and for the quiet proof — accumulating day by day — that this life is mine to live awake.
gratitude.closing: One ordinary day at a time. That's the whole thing.
